question: Should I commit to a weekly founder mastermind for the next year?
context: The group seems high-quality and could be energizing, but my calendar is already tight and I am trying to protect deep work time.
options_considered: Commit to the weekly mastermind for one year
Join for a shorter trial period
preferred_option: Commit to the weekly mastermind for one year
resource_commitment: Three hours weekly including travel and preparation
upside_case: The group sharpens decision quality, relationships, and accountability.
base_case: The group is useful but only marginally changes outcomes.
downside_case: The commitment fragments time, adds pressure, and displaces more valuable priorities.
known_risks: I tend to overcommit to future obligations
unknowns: How honest and high-signal the group actually is
Whether the recurring cadence will still feel valuable after a month
constraints: Need to preserve time for family and deep work
Do not want another commitment that becomes obligation theater
values_or_principles: Protect attention as a scarce resource
